extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ Primož won Stage 8 by attacking in the final kilometers after VINGEGAARD Jonas won the previous day's mountain stage, with both demonstrating Tour de France form.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ attacked despite going deep previous day, showing recovery capacity superior to typical stage race fatigue patterns.
VINGEGAARD won prior stage then finished ninth while supporting ROGLIČ, revealing Jumbo-Visma dual leadership flexibility pre-Tour.
ROLLAND Pierre targeted KOM from day one despite not needing breakaway presence, demonstrating selective energy management for secondary classifications.
15-rider breakaway with GESCHKE Simon (5:19 down, best-placed) given no distance, indicating peloton prioritized GC control over time gap.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: attacked again after previous day's deep effort, demonstrating exceptional recovery and Tour readiness
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: won previous stage then ninth today while supporting ROGLIČ, showing team-first mentality despite individual capability
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: positive: won white jersey and top-10 finish despite danger moment on final climb, showing composure under pressure
rider_signals.ROLLAND Pierre: positive: won KOM by 50 points over VERONA Carlos through calculated point-hunting from stage one
rider_signals.STORER Michael: positive: most aggressive rider despite failing to win, showing willingness to commit fully in break
rider_signals.BONNAMOUR Franck: negative: dropped from 15-rider breakaway, unable to sustain pace on climbs
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: neutral: sprinted to third on GC podium, maintaining position without attacking
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: neutral: second in young rider classification, dropping from higher position
